item.title: How do you think Miyazaki responded to this?
item.description: FP Edit: To be clear. AI is a tool. However, AI based on the work of artists and permission to use said art wasn't given is categorically bad. Most Viral Edit: thanks for the interaction! Send me pics! That's all. Edit2: This post is showing me a lot of people I can block as they have bad faith arguments. Thanks trolls.
item.dc:creator: bullsaint

item.title: My 3rd grade teacher was alarmed at my homework.
item.description: I kind of wish I could go back in time and see the look on my teacher's face as he awkwardly tries to deal with third grade me in this situation. With today being the first annual punch a Nazi day, I thought I'd share this fun anecdote from my childhood.
item.dc:creator: whatsisname

item.title: What he said!...This is a great question to get the transphobes on the back foot!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: Apparently it's my cake day... Have some math memes
item.description:
I like to put bad graphs up on the screen with no explanation and have my students figure out what's wrong
We then talk about the source of the graph, and possible motivations behind the graph
This one is not so much a meme as a depressing graph.
Sometimes graphs are just dumb, but we also talk about choosing the right type of graph for the data
This one is just all over the place. What in the world was the population of interest?
COVID times were full of terrible graphs
The person that made the vertical axis must have been on some good drugs
Even Canadians make bad graphs
item.dc:creator: acemathematician

item.title: Lucky mama and her baby rescued by the Gentle Barn
item.description: https://www.gentlebarn.org/donate/ - The Gentle Barn is a 501(c)3 nonprofit that runs on the generosity of donors. They are able to rescue animals and provide lifelong care for them, in species appropriate conditions. These are the lucky ones.

item.dc:creator: indomara

item.title: Eye didn't see that coming
item.description: Scared the shit out of me. My right eye is still extremely dilated, so eye am just waiting it out and hoping the Dr. is right. If it doesn't go back to normal in a couple hours eye might start to worry again. eye am genuinely trying to ward off a panic attack. So, yeah, PSA, don't accidentally get plant food in your eye, it creates a super freaky reaction. While eye have you here, wanna hear a joke? It will help me keep my mind off things. What do you call a snake thats 3.14 meters long? A P-eye-THON! Fuck I hope my eye is ok. It should be fine. The dr. said it would be fine... *** Update: The dilation went away, and now I just have a water drop sized red spot on my eye, which is far less intimidating than the grotesque uneven dilation thing. Vision is no longer blurred. Just a few natural tears here and there which feel very healthy. Per the Dr's orders I closed all my windows and wore sunglasses for the afternoon. At the moment, its hard to explain, but my eye just feels exausted, which is a very hard thing to find words for. Like, imagine if your eye had nausea, or a non visible bruise? Anyways, I think Im cool now. On the real though, thanks so much for caring. I was genuinely in panic attack mode for a minute there and freaked out. After those Dr phone calls I couldn't just sit here alone and cope without company.
item.dc:creator: METROlD

item.description: For clarification: I am super stoked for the new manager and have nothing against him. It's the company's mindset towards their current employees that I have an issue with
item.dc:creator: Longdickjohnson69

item.title: Missing a good friend today...
item.description: A1C Shaquille Hargrove was one of the best of us. Always quick with a joke and his laugh was infectious. No matter what went down on the Ops Floor, he was quick to get us back on track. Still missing you 11 years later, wingman.
item.dc:creator: xcanadiandevilx

item.title: The Woman With the Handbag
item.description: Didn't see this one yesterday so I figured I'd post it. Photo by Hans Runesson, Växjö, Sweden, 1985. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Woman_with_the_Handbag
item.dc:creator: OozyRatInASanitaryZoo

item.title: This sucks
item.description: Well, this sucks. I picked out my bulbs and decorations weeks ago and was super excited for some nice weather so I could get started gardening. I had little garden gnomes and ceramic mushrooms, and I got a bunch of tulip and lily bulbs. I went back the next day to water my plot, and the decorations had all been smashed. It was as if someone just tore through all the dirt, and about 80% of my bulbs were on the ground as well. It's super disappointing.
item.dc:creator: EowynsTerribleStew

item.dc:creator: CorgiWithAJetPack

item.title: This Memorial Day
item.description: My brother served as an Airman. He had the personal character and maturity at a young age to know the value of history laws and the guiding principles of virtue this Nation was striving for in our generation. In High School he wanted to be a teacher of History and was encouraged by a teacher to join the Air Force as a means to attain his goals. He had the discipline education and focus to see his ambitions through. He was able to understand Calculus in High School while I struggled with Algebra. He served 12 years in the Air Force on various rotations and cross training exercises ranging from North Korea as a presence in the room where North and South Korea hold their meetings. He cross trained in Kazakhstan and served most of his career on the field of Afghanistan as a TACP laser designator coordinating and calling in the targets for an airstrike with the A-10s in theater. Through the service he was able to find his beloved and wife where they both started and raised a family. For 12 years of going back and forth from one rotation to a leave for home where he could share his experiences with family sparing combat encounters our mom would always fear that it would be the last to ever hold or see him. Then finally when he was done with his career of 12 years. When he was back home and our mom could breathe a sigh of relief "my boys back home. He's safe" He goes and kills himself for what he felt he couldn't live with. For what he didn't want to say to me or our mother. So as a survivor of his suicide and my mother's grief in losing her first born son, I'd like to know and understand. Am I allowed to grieve for my brother? Am I allowed to grieve for the sacrifices he's made for this Country?
item.dc:creator: WoofWoof21
